free_text: Medium energy gamma rays following radiative capture
of 50 MeV polarized protons on 11B
Department of Physics and Laboratory of
Nuclear Studies
Department of Physics
Beam intensity is 15 to 30 nA.
- Target enrichment is 98.6%.
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness is 31 mg/cm**2.
- Target is self supported.
To separate gamma rays from neutrons
Research Center for Nuclear Physics
E-RSL: The overall resolution for 60 MeV gamma rays
was about 3.8% (delta-E/E), including 1% due to the
target thickness.
NaI crystal ensemble called HERMES
[T.Kishimoto, et al., Nucl.Instrum.Methods
Plastic scintillator shield as a Compton
suppressor
Sr + Am + On. Converted from NRDF D1618
ANG-CM is polar angle between beam and prompt gamma in
center of mass system
The systematic error for the absolute cross
sections due to the target thickness, the
efficiency of the HERMES, and the beam current
integration amouts to about 25%.
No information on source of uncertainties
Calculation given in Refs. [S.F.Tsai et al.,
Phys. Rev. Lett. 43(1979)576] and [M.Gari et
al., Phys. Rev. C18(1978)1071] in terms of both
the one-body and the two-body direct processes
